_comment: SCR repo-create isolation test (owner request 2026-06-15). Purpose: create a repository named 'test' on the EXISTING owner registry sample-nayvugfp (registry_id 'nayvugfp', derived from host [name-id].scr...) and record whether POST /v1/repositories 403s. This bypasses registry-create entirely to isolate the owner's hypothesis ('the 403 is from a disallowed registry-create option'). Uses the standard SCP_ACCESS_KEY/SECRET (no console SCR auth key needed for the SCP REST API). Broad expect_status so the real status/body is recorded even on 4xx; capture_soft + cleanup so a pre-existing 'test' (409, no id) is NOT deleted.

lifecycles._note: isolation probe — light, no VPC. list-registries first so the artifact shows every visible registry + its real id (safety net if 'nayvugfp' is wrong). Then create repo 'test' on registry_id=nayvugfp. 201/202 => repo-create works with access/secret (owner hypothesis confirmed: the earlier 403 was NOT a repo/registry permission). 403 => permission boundary on repo-create. 404/400 => registry_id wrong (use the id from list-registries).
